title: Epic: API ergonomics for cross-platform reuse
description:
Why: The jmap-chat library will be the shared foundation for jmap-chat-tui, jmap-chat-egui, jmap-chat-iced, jmap-chat-slint, and jmap-chat-tauri. Several API design problems were identified by reviewing the current code that will cause persistent boilerplate and friction in every UI adapter layer:

1. api_url + account_id threaded through every method (both come from Session)
2. Too-many-arguments on method calls — needs input structs
3. JmapChatClient is not Clone — forces manual Arc wrapping in every UI crate
4. UTCDate is opaque string — no parse() method, every UI crate re-implements RFC 3339 parsing
5. SetResponse.created is untyped serde_json::Value — not SetResponse<T>
6. filter_kind: Option<&str> instead of Option<ChatKind>
7. Message.sender_id is String instead of a typed SenderIdOrSelf enum
8. build_request hardcodes using array — cannot vary per capability

These must be resolved before the method epics build on top, or every method will inherit the bad shape.

Depends on: types.rs upgrades epic (jmap-chat-tui-1aq)
